campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary zoning rule and reviewed plans for a new bicycle bridge crossing over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth scholarship program
Council extends temporary zoning ordinance #2326
Hamilton Avenue bike bridge over Highway 17 moving forward with grant applications
Final feasibility study completed for bicycle crossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice opinions on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public feedback, the council decided to move forward with the measure by skipping the normal first reading step and planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance (city rule) being fast-tracked for approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to speed up the process

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to finalize several administrative matters and approve community initiatives. They wrapped up the 2026 weed abatement program, established new council operating procedures, approved affordable housing unit transfer rules, and authorized raising two pride flags (Progress Pride and Juneteenth). The council also heard from the regional transportation authority and reviewed plans for implementing the city's housing element.
keyTopics: Completed 2026 weed abatement program
New rules for affordable housing unit transfers
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
Regional transportation authority update
Housing element implementation planning

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several construction and service contracts for town projects. The council also voted to join a statewide insurance group to help manage the town's liability and insurance costs more efficiently.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc. for an unspecified project
Professional services agreement with Dudek (likely engineering or planning firm)
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority for better insurance coverage
Updates to existing agreements with NHA and an art transfer deal

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how the city is supporting kids and teens through various programs and services. They discussed youth mentorship initiatives, summer safety programs, library improvements, and how well code enforcement is working across the city.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and mentorship programs for teens
Safe Summer Initiative to keep kids engaged during summer break
Library facility upgrades and improving customer experience
Code Enforcement performance and annual results
Youth Intervention Services and how they're helping at-risk youth

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in-person and online via Zoom. Residents interested in speaking during the meeting could join the video call or phone line and use the 'raise hand' feature to request to comment on specific agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom (ID: 814 1954 3379) or phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for online and phone participants
Meeting conducted as hybrid format with in-person and virtual components

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, starting at 7 P.M. with a flag salute and personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group approved the official minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission regular meeting
Commissioner stories about Sunnyvale community experiences
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs and initiatives. The main topics included a presentation about a food-related budget program called Budget and Bites, an update on a youth organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization update
Summer jobs opportunities for Cupertino youth
Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 11
Public comment period on other city matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council is holding meetings that residents can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city is providing multiple ways for the public to participate and share their opinions, including submitting written comments before meetings.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Zoom meeting details (ID: 997-0675-9306)
Phone dial-in option available
eComment submission process for public input

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to handle several administrative and financial matters, including joining a state tax board program, deciding on fee waivers for a community educational organization, and approving construction contracts for city projects. The council also reviewed housing and community development plans and considered new ideas brought forward by individual council members.
keyTopics: Joining state Franchise Tax Board program with county partners
Fee waiver and donation decision for Milpitas Community Educational organization
Approval of construction project plans and contractor selection
Updates to housing and community development plans (2025-2030)
New project ideas submitted by council members for future consideration

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss the city's natural gas utility finances and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision was whether to approve changes to residential and commercial gas service rates, along with adjustments to the utility's financial reserves and budget.
keyTopics: Natural gas rate changes for residential customers (Rate Schedule G-1)
Commercial and large business gas rate adjustments (Schedules G-2 and G-3)
City gas utility financial forecast and reserve transfers for fiscal year 2027
Approval of previous meeting minutes from the Utilities Advisory Commission
